Story: The Rope of Unity
Once, a traveler saw three men trying to pull a heavy stone. Each pulled from a different side, and the stone did not move. Then, they tied their ropes together and pulled in the same direction. To their surprise, the stone moved with ease.

The traveler said: “Alone you are weak, but together you are unbreakable.”

Why Middle Eastern Unity Matters
	1.	Shared Resources – Water, energy, and trade routes are blessings for the whole region. Together, countries can protect and develop them fairly.
	2.	Economic Power – When the Middle East works as one, it becomes stronger in global markets, reducing dependence on outsiders.
	3.	Cultural and Religious Bonds – The region shares faith, history, and traditions that naturally connect its people.
	4.	Peace and Stability – Division invites outside interference, but unity builds security from within.
	5.	Global Respect – A united Middle East has a stronger voice in international organizations and can shape its own future.

The lesson: Breaking unity weakens the whole region. Preserving partnerships is not only wisdom — it is also a responsibility, so that future generations inherit strength, not division.

What form will the mahr take? It can be money, property, jewelry, or even an act of service, such as teaching the bride a part of the Quran.

What is the value of the mahr? The value should be mutually agreed upon and specified clearly in the marriage contract. The amount should be reasonable and within the groom's financial means, keeping in mind that the purpose is not to create a financial burden.

How does the mahr relate to a cultural "dowry"? The mahr is different from a dowry, which is a gift given by the bride's family to the groom. It is a gift given directly to the bride, and confusing the two can lead to misconceptions about the woman's value.

You matter to me, and you inspire me.
I observe your gestures, your pauses, your laughter and your sadness… and with each passing day I learn from your culture and your traditions.
I want you to know that I am proud of you. Maybe many don’t tell you often, or perhaps they don’t notice, but I do see in you that passion and effort you put into life and into everything that matters to you. I see your constant work to overcome, to become a better person every single day.
Your strength and dedication to being a great leader and minister lift you up for your people.
The love you give to your children, your family, your friends, and your country—all of it makes you a great human being.
Never, ever doubt the amazing person you are: absolutely beautiful in soul and heart, noble and admired, a man with vision and success who inspires.
I wish for all your dreams to come into your life with success and prosperity, with the blessing of God and the universe.
I cross your shadow and remain with you; I embrace your happy days and also your grey ones, when they cannot shine.
I value the sacred art of listening to you, I quietly gather your tears if you need to cry, and I applaud your smiles just to see you happy.
I seek your fire, your way of loving, and in the middle of chaos I find refuge in your calm, because loving you entirely is my only peace.
Finding someone who ignites the fire within our soul is magical, like stumbling upon a rare gem in a sea of common stones. It is a connection that transcends the surface and touches the deepest corners of being. Because the one who sees the essence, the dreams, the truth in the eyes… also inspires us to grow, to evolve, to become the best version of ourselves, and yet still accepts us as we are, with our light and our shadows.
And if you ever ask what I feel for you, seek no more answers and never doubt me: my gaze alone is enough.

حضرة سمو الشيخ حمدان بن محمد بن راشد آل مكتوم (حفظكم الله ورعاكم)
أتمنى من الله تعالى أن تكونوا في خيرٍ وصحةٍ وعافيةٍ دائمة، وأن يزيدكم الله عز وجل رفعةً وكرامةً وبركةً في عمركم وأعمالكم.
أنا من باكستان، ووالدي إمام مسجد وراتبه الشهري قليل جدًا (حوالي ٢٠ ألف روبية)، ومن الصعب علينا أن نغطي به مصاريف البيت. الحمد لله نحن من آل بيت رسول الله ﷺ (سادة)، ولنا شوق كبير منذ زمن طويل لأداء العمرة، ولكن ظروفنا المادية لا تسمح لنا.
إذا تفضّلتم بكرمكم وطيبة قلوبكم وساعدتمونا لتحقيق هذه الأمنية، فسيكون ذلك فضلًا عظيمًا وإحسانًا كبيرًا لن ننساه أبدًا. نحن عائلة صغيرة من ستة أشخاص فقط، وإن لم يكن ممكنًا للجميع، فرجائي أن يذهب والداي على الأقل (أمي وأبي) لأداء العمرة.
جزاكم الله خير الجزاء، وبارك فيكم وفي أهلكم الكرام، ورفع قدركم في الدنيا والآخرة، وجعل ذلك في ميزان حسناتكم.🤍
